compiled_state.title: Completion verifier re-checks already-closed DDs at epic level using stricter interpretation than story verifier
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: When story 536e-3683 was closed at 7/8 DDs (DD6: 5-SC integration test deferred as architecturally incompatible with behavioral testing standard Rule 5), the epic completion verifier still flagged the corresponding SC7 as FAIL using the same criterion. The story verifier accepted the structural boundary test rationale; the epic verifier did not. This caused an extra unplanned remediation task (adding test_gap_detection_routes_by_child_type) and an additional review/commit cycle. Fix: when all child stories are closed with the verifier's PASS verdict, the epic verifier should trust those story-level verdicts rather than re-verifying each DD from scratch. Alternatively, the epic verifier prompt should note that story-level verifier PASSes are authoritative for their DDs.

comments.body: Classification: llm-behavioral — dual-signal confirmed: (1) ticket describes epic verifier re-checking DDs more strictly than story verifier accepted, (2) affected file is plugins/dso/agents/completion-verifier.md. Proceeding with bot-psychologist inline investigation (fallback: Agent tool context is orchestrator-level but bot-psychologist requires direct user interaction loop; applying inline taxonomy analysis per fix-bug SKILL inline fallback).
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Fix implemented: added Step 1.5 (Load Child Story Verdicts — Epics Only) to completion-verifier.md. The step instructs the epic verifier to treat closed child stories as inherited PASS for their SCs, and only re-verify SCs where no child story is closed. Step 2 updated to scope to non-inherited criteria only. 4 behavioral assertion tests written in tests/skills/test-completion-verifier-story-trust.sh (structural boundary tests per Rule 5). All tests pass GREEN.
comments.author: Test
